title: The effects of state paid sick leave mandates on parental childcare time

construct_vars: gvar_tbl <- df %>% dplyr::filter(pslm_state_lag2 == 1) %>% dplyr::group_by(fips) %>% dplyr::summarise(first_t = min(time), .groups='drop')
df <- df %>% dplyr::left_join(gvar_tbl, by='fips') %>% dplyr::mutate(gvar_CS = ifelse(!is.na(first_t), as.numeric(first_t), 0))
cs_construct_vars: df <- df %>% dplyr::group_by(fips, year) %>% dplyr::summarise(carehh_k = weighted.mean(carehh_k, wt06, na.rm=TRUE), wt06 = sum(wt06, na.rm=TRUE), gvar_month = dplyr::first(gvar_CS), .groups='drop') %>% dplyr::mutate(gvar_CS = ifelse(gvar_month > 0, as.integer(floor((gvar_month - 1) / 12) + 2003), 0L))

notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Maclean & Pabilonia (2025) JHE. IMPORTANT: Paper's MAIN estimator is Gardner (did2s), NOT TWFE. TWFE is reported only in Appendix Table 10 as one of 5 alternative estimators (Gardner, BJS, Wooldridge, Stacked DiD, TWFE). Appendix Table 10 TWFE: reghdfe carehh_k pslm_state_lag2 controls [pw=wt06], a(fips time) cl(fips). RCS data (139K obs, 78K with hh_child==1). Monthly time (239 periods), 44 states, 10 treated states (6 yearly cohorts: 2014,2017,2018,2019,2020,2022). CS-DID segfaults with monthly RCS — use yearly collapsed panel for CS: CS-NT=-1.61 (SE 4.20), CS-NYT=-1.25 (SE 4.22), both insignificant. Paper uses 'carehh_k' = primary childcare minutes per average day. SIGN REVERSAL: TWFE=+4.45** vs CS-NT=-1.61 (ns). Bacon decomposition shows cohort ~2020 (ATT=+10.12, weight=34%) drives positive TWFE — confounded with COVID lockdowns (PSL lagged 2yr → 2018 adoption activates 2020). Cohorts 2017 and 2022 show negative ATTs (-3.97 and -21.49). GARDNER DIAGNOSTIC: Gardner agrees with TWFE (both positive early post-treatment, negative at late horizons). NOT sensitive to time granularity (monthly≈yearly). IS sensitive to endpoint binning: bin at h=+7 collapses h=7(+10) with h=8-9(-41) → spurious -25 drop. h≥7 is 100% Cohort 2014 (CT, N=33) — pure noise. Cohort-specific Gardner: wildly heterogeneous (2014: +16 to -57; 2017: -3 to -18; 2020: +4 to +15 with SE~10; 2022: -6 to -15). TWFE/Gardner agreement explained by shared cohort-size weighting (Cohort 2017=40%, 2020=25% dominate), while CS uses uniform group×time weighting. Conclusion: sign reversal is real — driven by COVID-confounded Cohort 2020 dominating size-weighted estimators (TWFE, Gardner) while CS distributes weight more evenly.
